note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the use of transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) to investigate its effects on animal subjects, specifically mice. The goal is likely to explore the impact of tDCS on various physiological or behavioral parameters.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "Sterilization of necessary materials and instruments, including autoclaving for cotton/plastic-based materials and dry heat sterilization for metallic surgical instruments.",
    "Anesthesia of mice using ketamine and xylazine, followed by monitoring their temperature and ensuring they are anesthetized.",
    "Placement of electrodes on the mouse's head and attachment to a stimulator.",
    "Adjustment of the thermal platform to maintain a controlled temperature of 37°C."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Mice as the experimental subjects.",
    "Animal transcranial DC Stimulator for delivering electrical currents.",
    "Sterilized materials and instruments for conducting the experiment.",
    "Ketamine and xylazine for anesthesia."
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Animal transcranial DC Stimulator with adjustable settings.",
    "Thermal platform for maintaining a constant temperature.",
    "Electrodes attached to the mouse's head.",
    "Sterilized materials and instruments for preparation and handling."
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Temperature controller set at 37°C.",
    "Graph showing mean contact quality over multiple tDCS sessions."
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Transition from sterilization and anesthesia preparation to the actual application of tDCS on the mouse.",
    "Transition from setting up the equipment to recording the results."
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Mean contact quality measurements over different tDCS sessions.",
    "Visual indicators of the mouse's response to tDCS, such as behavior or physiological responses."
  ],
